description: Trigger and dispatch evals for the rlat skill. Tests (1) does the rlat skill fire on rlat-CLI workflow signals, (2) does it correctly identify which sub-workflow applies (init / refresh / search / skill-context / memory / compare / convert / optimise), (3) does it correctly DEFER to the deep-research skill on cross-file synthesis questions, (4) does it stay silent on tasks better served by Read/Edit/Grep or by other vector-DB tools. Runnable with any skill-eval harness that understands the {prompt, expected_behavior} shape.

evals.prompt: I just cloned this repo. Set up rlat for it.
expected_behavior: Identifies this as the Init workflow.
Runs `rlat init-project` (or `rlat install-encoder` first if encoder isn't installed).
Verifies the `.rlat` and `.claude/resonance-context.md` artefacts exist after the run.
Surfaces next-step paths: `rlat search` for one-shot, `deep-research` skill for multi-hop.

evals.prompt: I've changed a bunch of files in src/. The rlat knowledge model is probably stale. Fix it.
expected_behavior: Identifies this as the Refresh workflow.
Runs `rlat refresh <km>.rlat` (NOT `rlat sync` — that's remote-mode only).
After refresh, runs `rlat profile <km>.rlat` to verify drift is 0.
If `.claude/resonance-context.md` was generated from this KM, regenerates it.

evals.prompt: What does the encoder pooling strategy do in this codebase?
expected_behavior: Recognizes a single-hop fact question against the indexed corpus.
Runs `rlat search <km>.rlat "encoder pooling strategy" --top-k 5 --format context --mode augment`.
Cites the specific source files (e.g. `src/resonance_lattice/field/encoder.py`) inline.
Does NOT run grep first — semantic search is the right starting point for conceptual questions.

evals.prompt: I need to know the exact retention policy for compliance reporting. No hallucination — the answer must come from the corpus or be a refusal.
expected_behavior: Recognizes the compliance-grade safety requirement.
Runs `rlat search <km>.rlat "<question>" --format context --mode constrain --strict-names`.
Explains `constrain` mode tells the LLM to refuse on thin evidence (2.0% bench-measured hallucination).
Does NOT default to `augment` for compliance-stakes queries.

evals.prompt: I'm writing an Anthropic skill that needs to inject Fabric documentation context into the prompt. How do I wire that up?
expected_behavior: Identifies this as the Skill-context workflow.
Generates a `!command` block using `rlat skill-context fabric-docs.rlat --query "<preset>" --query "$user_query" --top-k 5`.
Notes that preset queries come first; user-query last (token-budget truncation drops later first).
Suggests `--strict --strict-names --mode constrain` for compliance-grade skills.

evals.prompt: Have we discussed encoder selection rationale in past sessions? What did we decide?
expected_behavior: Identifies this as the Memory recall workflow.
Runs `rlat memory --memory-root ".claude/memory" recall "encoder selection rationale"`.
Surfaces ranked memory entries fused across working/episodic/semantic tiers.
Cites entry IDs in the answer.

evals.prompt: Compare project.rlat against the fabric-docs.rlat knowledge model — what does our project know that the public Fabric corpus doesn't?
expected_behavior: Identifies this as the Compare workflow.
Runs `rlat compare project.rlat fabric-docs.rlat` (always uses base band).
Reports centroid cosine + asymmetric mutual coverage.
For deeper analysis, suggests follow-up RQL ops (corpus_diff, evidence).

evals.prompt: I want to ship this knowledge model to a colleague who doesn't have access to my source files. Make it self-contained.
expected_behavior: Identifies this as the Convert workflow (target = bundled).
Runs `rlat convert <km>.rlat --to bundled`.
Notes that conversion validates content_hash against live bytes; aborts on drift.
Suggests `rlat refresh` first if the validation fails.

evals.prompt: Why did we pick gte-modernbert-base as the default encoder over BGE-large or E5? I want the full rationale across the codebase.
expected_behavior: Recognizes this as a multi-hop synthesis question requiring evidence across multiple files.
DEFERS to the `deep-research` skill rather than running `rlat search` once.
Does NOT try to handle the synthesis with a single-shot retrieval.

evals.prompt: Summarise the trade-offs we've considered for store mode design across all the design docs.
expected_behavior: Recognizes this as a cross-file synthesis question — "summarise … across".
DEFERS to the `deep-research` skill.
Does NOT default to single-shot `rlat search`.

evals.prompt: Can we lift retrieval recall on our private Fabric knowledge model? It's natural-language Q&A.
expected_behavior: Identifies this as the Optimise workflow.
Surfaces the cost upfront ($2-8 + Kaggle T4 time) and the regression risk (2 of 3 publicly-tested corpora regressed).
Runs `rlat optimise <km>.rlat --estimate` first for a per-corpus cost preview.
If the user proceeds, runs `rlat optimise <km>.rlat --corpus-description "<one-line>"` with a meaningful description.
Notes the API key requirement and points at docs/user/API_KEYS.md if not set.

evals.prompt: Rename the `EncoderConfig` class to `EncoderSettings` across the repo and update the imports.
expected_behavior: Recognizes this as an exact-symbol rename — uses `Grep "EncoderConfig"` and `Edit`, not `rlat search`.
Does NOT invoke the rlat skill — semantic search adds nothing for deterministic symbol rewrites.

evals.prompt: Add a `print(f"debug: {query}")` right before the `encoder.encode(query)` call in src/resonance_lattice/field/encoder.py:135.
expected_behavior: Uses Read + Edit on the specific file.
Does NOT invoke rlat — the user already supplied the exact location.

evals.prompt: How do I set up Pinecone for my embedding store? I want to use their hosted index.
expected_behavior: Recognizes this is about a different product entirely (Pinecone), not rlat.
Does NOT invoke the rlat skill.
Answers the Pinecone question directly or directs the user to Pinecone docs.
Recognizes this is answerable from training alone, no rlat involved.
Does NOT invoke the rlat skill.
Answers `Paris` directly.
